description: §41.15.8 — schemaFor(User) called in an EVENT-HANDLER interpolation position (a <button onclick=${ schemaFor(User) }>). This is 'any other markup context' outside a <schema> block, so it SHALL fire E-SCHEMAFOR-INVALID-CALL-CONTEXT. Regression guard: the invalid-context walker (Pass B) previously never reached event-handler expression nodes, so the call compiled clean and leaked a runtime schemaFor() call (compile-only; throws at runtime) into client.js.
rationale: §41.15.8 normative statement: schemaFor(...) calls outside a <schema> block — including inside any other markup context — SHALL emit E-SCHEMAFOR-INVALID-CALL-CONTEXT. The function-call form is canonical INSIDE <schema> blocks only (its output is a table-declaration fragment that requires the <schema> parser context). An event-handler interpolation is one such invalid markup context. Resolution: wrap the call inside a <schema>${ schemaFor(...) }</> block.
